title: cistus clusii var. fastigiatus
intro: Cistus clusii var. fastigiatus, also known as Rock Rose, is a variety of the Cistus clusii plant, a small, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its leaves, flowers, and roots possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The Rock Rose plant has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, as well as to treat digestive issues and menstrual problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or cream to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
